pdg_node.statement: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float, detector_1: NDArray, fs: float) -> NDArray
pdg_node.informal_desc: Classifies each of the four base SQIs into discrete categories (0=unqualified, 1=suspicious, 2=optimal) based on a set of heuristic rules, some of which are adaptive to heart rate.

declaration.name: classifysqisheuristically
declaration.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float, detector_1: NDArray, fs: float) -> NDArray
declaration.docstring: Classifies each of the four base SQIs into discrete categories (0=unqualified, 1=suspicious, 2=optimal) based on a set of heuristic rules, some of which are adaptive to heart rate.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Aggregates the discrete classifications of the SQIs by counting classes to determine the final signal quality label ('Unacceptable', 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable').

declaration.name: aggregateheuristicclasses
declaration.type_signature: (class_matrix: NDArray) -> str
declaration.docstring: Aggregates the discrete classifications of the SQIs by counting classes to determine the final signal quality label ('Unacceptable', 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable').

declaration.name: aggregateheuristicclasses

declaration.docstring: Aggregates the discrete classifications of the SQIs by counting classes to determine the final signal quality label ('Unacceptable', 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable').

pdg_node.statement: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float) -> NDArray
pdg_node.informal_desc: Transforms each of the four raw SQI scores into a vector of three fuzzy membership grades corresponding to 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable', and 'Unacceptable' quality levels.

declaration.name: calculatefuzzymemberships
declaration.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float) -> NDArray
declaration.docstring: Transforms each of the four raw SQI scores into a vector of three fuzzy membership grades corresponding to 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable', and 'Unacceptable' quality levels.

declaration.name: calculatefuzzymemberships
declaration.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float) -> NDArray
declaration.docstring: Transforms each of the four raw SQI scores into a vector of three fuzzy membership grades corresponding to 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable', and 'Unacceptable' quality levels.

declaration.name: calculatefuzzymemberships
declaration.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float) -> NDArray
declaration.docstring: Transforms each of the four raw SQI scores into a vector of three fuzzy membership grades corresponding to 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable', and 'Unacceptable' quality levels.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Combines the fuzzy membership vectors using a weighted-average evaluation matrix to compute a final score (V), which is then mapped to a quality label.

declaration.name: aggregatefuzzymemberships
declaration.type_signature: (evaluation_matrix_R: NDArray) -> str
declaration.docstring: Combines the fuzzy membership vectors using a weighted-average evaluation matrix to compute a final score (V), which is then mapped to a quality label.

declaration.docstring: Combines the fuzzy membership vectors using a weighted-average evaluation matrix to compute a final score (V), which is then mapped to a quality label.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Calculates a Signal Quality Index (kSQI) based on the statistical kurtosis of the signal.

declaration.name: calculatekurtosissqi
declaration.type_signature: (signal: NDArray, fisher: bool) -> float
declaration.docstring: Calculates a Signal Quality Index (kSQI) based on the statistical kurtosis of the signal.
declaration.conceptual_summary: MeasureDistributionTailedness. Computes the fourth standardized moment of a 1D numerical array. This scalar value quantifies the 'tailedness' of the distribution, measuring the propensity for values to be concentrated far from the mean (outliers) relative to a Gaussian distribution. A boolean flag selects between the raw moment and the excess moment (adjusted by a constant).. Applications: Quantitative Finance: Assessing the tail risk of an asset's return distribution. High kurtosis indicates a greater probability of extreme market movements than predicted by simpler models., Industrial Process Control: Monitoring sensor data for anomalies. A change in the kurtosis of pressure or temperature readings can signal a deviation from stable operation and predict system failures., Geophysics: Characterizing the impulsiveness of seismic signals. The kurtosis of ground acceleration time-series helps differentiate between background noise and sharp, high-impact tremor events., Neuroscience: Analyzing neuronal firing patterns. The kurtosis of inter-spike interval distributions can distinguish between regular, rhythmic firing and bursty, irregular activity.

declaration.docstring: Calculates a Signal Quality Index (kSQI) based on the statistical kurtosis of the signal.
